name: Design Standards Vocabulary
description: Controlled vocabulary for the design standards domain spanning product, interface, and API design.

terms.definition: A documented, enforceable rule for how design and engineering deliverables must be produced.
A collection of related design standards covering naming, formatting, and presentation.
A versioned set of components, tokens, and patterns implementing the design standards.
Standards specifically governing API design, evolution, and operation.
Machine-readable contract such as OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, or JSON Schema.
Tool that mechanically checks artifacts against design standards.
Open-source linter for OpenAPI and AsyncAPI specifications.
IETF document defining requirement-level keywords (MUST, SHOULD, MAY).
International standard for date and time representations.
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines defining accessibility conformance levels.
RFC 7807 standard format for HTTP API error responses.
Versioning convention encoding compatibility intent in MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.
Standardized rules describing how API or design surfaces are retired.
Named, versioned design value (color, spacing, typography) consumed by implementations.
Stated level of compliance with accessibility standards such as WCAG.
Group accountable for owning, reviewing, and approving changes to the standards.
Documented procedure for proposing, reviewing, and ratifying changes to the standards.
